div.head: The Ambassador in the Dominican Republic (Warren) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Ciudad Trujillo, November 25, 1943—8 a.m.
[Received November 26—5 p.m.]

div.p:
A–611. For Foreign Economic Administration.The Office of Economic Warfare, formerly the Board of Economic Warfare, was consolidated into the Foreign Economic Administration, September 25, 1943. As requested by Department’s circular airgram of November 13, 1943Ante, p. 131. the Embassy
reports that the additional simplifications of export controls referred to in the above airgram have been communicated to the Dominican Import-Export Control Committee and that the Committee has expressed its acceptance of and complete agreement with such proposals.
In accordance with the Department’s instructions, the revised procedure will become effective in the Dominican Republic on January 1, 1944.
